title: VOA — Dialed In, Pacific Adventure, Vision Health, Nuclear Fusion
content: Section 1: 'dialed in' tech idioms · Section 2: Australian Navy rescues Pacific rower · Section 3: avoiding computer vision trouble · Section 4: France's nuclear fusion record
attribution: Audio courtesy of VOA Learning English (learningenglish.voanews.com) — public domain. Questions © IELTS Band AI.
license: public domain (US federal government work, commercial reuse permitted with attribution)

questions.question: A ________ is a control used to tune in to a channel on a radio or television.
To be 'dialed in' or 'tuned in' to a project means to be ________.
What does the expression 'don't touch that dial' mean?
A device that turns an electrical circuit on or off is called a ________.
The idiom 'to flip a ________' means to make something happen quickly and easily.
What does the idiom 'to move the needle' most nearly mean?
options: A. To cause a noticeable, usually positive, change
B. To make a very small mistake
C. To slow something down on purpose
D. To threaten someone with a weapon
questions.answer: A. To cause a noticeable, usually positive, change

questions.question: In the host's example, a restaurant changing to all organic food slowly was 'not a matter of flipping a ________'.
What is 'static' in the context of older radios?
options: A. Noise produced in a receiver by atmospheric or electrical disturbances
B. The volume control of a radio
D. A musical pause between songs
questions.answer: A. Noise produced in a receiver by atmospheric or electrical disturbances

questions.question: On older measuring devices, the small pointer that shows a reading is called a ________.
Which expression also means 'keep watching or listening'?
The rescued rower is ________ Mockus from Lithuania.
At the time of rescue, the rower was ________ years old.
From which city did the rower set out in October 2024?
His intended destination was the Australian city of ________.
His planned solo crossing covered about ________ kilometres.
What was the name of the cyclone that struck his boat?
When rescued, he was about ________ kilometres off the coast of Queensland.
Which navy carried out the rescue?
options: C. The Royal New Zealand Navy

questions.question: Who was the first person to row the Pacific solo, though towed the last 48 km in 1983?
Who became the first woman to row solo across the Pacific in 2023?
About ________ million working-age Americans spend more than seven hours a day in front of digital screens.
The figure comes from the American ________ Association.
Which of the following is NOT listed as a vision problem linked to heavy screen use?
What does the 20-20-20 rule recommend?
options: A. Every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds
B. Use a screen for no more than 20 minutes at a time, 20 times a day
C. Take a 20-minute walk every 20 days for 20 weeks
D. Blink 20 times every 20 minutes for 20 seconds
questions.answer: A. Every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds

questions.question: For children, the rule is extended to '20-20-20-2', meaning an extra ________ hours of outdoor play per day.
What is a normal blinking rate, according to the report?
options: A. About 18 to 22 times per minute
B. About 3 to 7 times per minute
C. About 40 to 50 times per minute
questions.answer: A. About 18 to 22 times per minute

questions.question: Blinking rate at a screen drops to about how many times per minute?
The eye condition linked to screen use that mainly affects children is ________, or nearsightedness.
What does the American Academy of Ophthalmology say about blue-light glasses?
options: A. They are not very effective
B. They cure myopia in children
C. They should be worn at night
D. They are essential for all computer users
questions.answer: A. They are not very effective

questions.question: How long before bedtime do experts recommend stopping screen use?
French scientists sustained hydrogen plasma for a record ________ seconds.
That duration is about ________ minutes.
The record-setting experiment took place on 12 February ________.
By how much did the French team beat the previous Chinese record?
The organisation that ran the experiment is France's ________ Energy Commission, known as the CEA.
The doughnut-shaped device used to confine the plasma with magnets is called a ________.
Inside the device, hydrogen was heated to about ________ million degrees Celsius.
For fusion to actually produce more energy than it consumes, plasma temperatures must exceed:
What principle does nuclear fusion mimic?
options: A. The energy reaction at the centre of the Sun
B. The chemical reaction inside a battery
C. The splitting of heavy uranium atoms
D. The combustion of natural gas
questions.answer: A. The energy reaction at the centre of the Sun

questions.question: When is the multinational ITER tokamak project in southern France now expected to begin operations?
